name: Alpha Tours Japan
aliases: アルファツアーズジャパン
patterns: Fake guide — actually just a driver
Forces you to leave 5-star reviews (grabs your phone)
Lies about credentials
Pockets parking fees

name: Japan Custom Tours
aliases: ジャパンカスタムツアーズ
patterns: Demands bank wire transfer only
Goes silent after payment
Reported loss of $2,260

name: JAPAN PASSION
patterns: Fake travel agency
No company address or phone number
Wire transfer only
Invoices with no logo or branding

name: Kinkakuji Volunteer Guides
aliases: 金閣寺ボランティアガイド
patterns: Pose as free volunteer guides, then demand tips or force you to buy things
Hang around the Kinkakuji bus stop area
Kyoto city has posted official warning signs about them

name: Kabukicho Bar Scam (Bottakuri)
patterns: Street touts lure tourists into bars with promises of cheap all-you-can-drink deals
Final bill is 5-10x higher than quoted price with hidden 'seat charges'
Intimidation tactics used when customers refuse to pay
Some bars block exits until victims pay
January 2024: 15 people arrested for fraudulent bar operations in Kabukicho

name: Roppongi Drink Spiking Scam
patterns: Friendly strangers or touts invite tourists to bars in Roppongi
Drinks are spiked with drugs causing unconsciousness
Credit cards stolen or charged thousands of dollars while victim is unconscious
U.S. Embassy has issued official warnings about this scam

name: Fake Monk Scam
patterns: Person dressed as Buddhist monk approaches tourists at Ueno, Asakusa, and tourist areas
Gives a 'blessed' bracelet or charm, then demands donation of 10,000-20,000 yen
Shows fake donation book with inflated amounts to pressure victims
Real monks NEVER approach tourists to sell items or solicit donations on the street

name: Shirotaku (Unlicensed Taxi)
patterns: Unlicensed private cars (white license plates) solicit tourists at Haneda, Narita airports
No meter — charge arbitrary inflated prices, often 3-5x normal fare
No insurance coverage if accident occurs
Legal taxis have GREEN license plates — always check

name: Charity Petition / Donation Scam
patterns: Well-dressed people approach tourists in Shibuya, Shinjuku, Harajuku claiming to collect for disaster relief
Show fake official documents and fabricated donation receipts with large amounts
Pressure tourists to donate 5,000-10,000 yen or more
Money goes directly to scammers, not to any charity

name: Fake Ticket Resale (Ghibli, Nintendo, USJ)
patterns: Websites sell 'Ghibli Museum tickets' at $50-100+ when official price is ~1,000 yen
Tickets are fake or have wrong name (Ghibli requires ID matching ticket name)
Claim 'guaranteed availability' even when officially sold out
Same scam applies to Nintendo Museum, USJ Express Passes
Victims denied entry at gate and cannot get refund

name: Booking.com Fake Hotel Listing
patterns: Fake hotels listed on Booking.com with stolen photos from legitimate properties
Tourists arrive to find abandoned houses or non-existent addresses
2023 Chiba case: tourist paid 33,000 yen for 'luxury villa' that was an abandoned house
Additional unrecoverable trip costs (car rental, transport) totaling ~100,000 yen

name: Dotonbori / Namba Street Tout Scam
patterns: Street touts in Dotonbori and Namba lure tourists into overpriced bars and restaurants
Promise cheap deals but final bill includes massive hidden charges
Some touts specifically target foreign tourists with English solicitation
Intimidation if customers challenge the bill

name: Train Station 'Lost Wallet' Beggar
patterns: Foreigner boards busy trains claiming to have lost wallet and needs money for airport
Asks for 5,000-10,000 yen claiming it's an emergency
Operates repeatedly on the same train lines
Reported multiple times on Reddit r/JapanTravel and r/japanlife

name: Tourist Price Gouging (Dual Pricing)
patterns: Some restaurants charge higher prices to foreign tourists than to Japanese customers
Separate 'English menu' with inflated prices compared to Japanese menu
Adding unexplained charges to bills for foreign customers
Overcharging at drugstores by scanning wrong items

name: Illegal Airbnb / Vacation Rental
patterns: Unlicensed properties listed on Airbnb operating illegally under Japan's minpaku law
Host asks guests to 'sneak in' during security guard shift changes
Fake listings with stolen photos — property doesn't exist
Host pressures to pay via PayPal/bank transfer instead of through platform

name: Nightlife Credit Card Fraud ($6,000 scam)
patterns: Documented by YouTuber Chris Broad (Abroad in Japan) — tourist lost $6,000 in one night
Combination of bar hopping with touts, spiked drinks, and credit card fraud
Touts lead victims to multiple venues, each adding charges
Credit card charged repeatedly at different establishments while victim is intoxicated

name: JTB Corporation
patterns: Japan's largest travel agency, established 1912
Licensed and regulated by Japan Tourism Agency
Official partner for many Japanese attractions and events
Offices throughout Japan and internationally

name: H.I.S. Co., Ltd.
patterns: Major Japanese travel agency founded 1980
Licensed by Japan Tourism Agency
Physical stores across Japan for in-person booking
Known for affordable tour packages and flight deals

name: Klook
patterns: Hong Kong-based activity booking platform, largest in Asia
Secure encrypted payment processing
Official partner with many Japanese attractions for ticket sales
Good for JR passes, attraction tickets, and activity bookings

name: Viator (by TripAdvisor)
patterns: Owned by TripAdvisor, one of the world's largest tour booking platforms
Large verified review pool
Free cancellation on many bookings
Legitimate — but always check individual tour operator reviews
